Grim Pauldrons

Slot: SHOULDERS
AC: 13
STR: +10
WT: 4.5 Size: MEDIUM
Class: WAR CLR PAL SHD BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Grim Pauldrons
Lore Item No Trade
Slot: SHOULDERS
AC: 13
STR: +10
WT: 4.5 Size: MEDIUM
Class: WAR CLR PAL SHD BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Type:Armor
Stackable:No

Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 45

I would like to concur on this one, I got them 2 nights ago hunting for Seb Keys.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Sep 07 2001 at 10:58 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) lol who cares if its grim lol, whoever says can wear it can wear it
#Anonymous, Posted: May 17 2001 at 4:34 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Why would a Paladin be able to wear something GRIM?? Hmmm?? That would make him/her an SK then. :) Oh .. yep SKs can wear. :)
RE: Grim = Evil
QuoteReply
# Aug 04 2001 at 12:48 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248488665
15 posts
Umm so? Lots of weapons and stuff that have odd names to them and paladins could use.... and the fact that clerics could use it... I mean overall there is no such thing as an EVIL cleric.. no matter how you see it.. yes it's a roleplaying game.. but doesn't mean there can't be evil paladins.. roleplaying is how you roleplay, doesn't matter what you are really.
▲ Page top
RE: Grim = Evil
QuoteReply
# Mar 07 2003 at 11:24 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591765923498
Guru
170 posts
Quote:
but doesn't mean there can't be evil paladins


Completely and totally wrong. You were half-right with your argument. Remember this is a video game and generally that in itself is all the excuse needed to blur the lines between fact and fiction. However, Paladin, the word "Paladin", represents good and the epitome of chivalry.

Their opposites are Dark Paladins, or in EQ words, the Shadow Knight (who don't have the healing touch). There is no such thing as an evil Paladin. Any Paladin who heads in the wrong direction on the road will lose his or her's powers immediately and become an ordinary fighter. That said, your RP argument is null.


By the way, Grim does not necessarily mean evil. Grim can be stern and unyielding and most Paladins, the fanatical ones, are of such traits.
▲ Page top
RE: Grim = Evil
QuoteReply
# Aug 23 2005 at 11:43 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592278505390
Scholar
20 posts
Nope, he is completely right.

Do you think a Dark Elf would consider himself "evil"? Nope.

In EQ the "evil" gods can grant the same healing powers as the good ones (clerics of Inny, Bertox, Cazic), so I always thought they should be able to have paladins serving them as well. Yes, evil Paladins not anti-paladins, or Shadowknights as they are called.

An evil character can follow a code of honor or chivalry as logically as a good one. In DnD terms you'd call that a "lawful evil" character.
▲ Page top
RE: Grim = Evil
QuoteReply
# Jan 08 2004 at 3:15 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591788464635
45 posts
Appearently you've never played on a pvp server... Trust me, there is such a thing as an "evil pallidan." So what does it mean when an elven race pally is killed by a human pally or dwarven pally?
▲ Page top
RE: Grim = Evil
QuoteReply
# Mar 07 2003 at 9:36 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591776013048
View User Forum
263 posts
paladins ARE "inherently "good."" "A paragon of chivalry; a heroic champion"
▲ Page top
RE: Grim = Evil
QuoteReply
# Sep 02 2001 at 5:27 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591760647436
9 posts
This is platemail, meaning bards, warriors, Sk's, clerics and pally's may wear it.
But its what I like to call "evil" equipment, so "good" classes cant wear (hence no bards and pallys) (and yes there are a number of "good" equipments as well). This leaves it with shadow knights, warriors and clerics. Why? because unlike paladins and bards, these class's aren't inherently "good" (dark elf, human and erudite clerics can be evil to my knowledge, and many warriors can be evil. And all sk's are evil)
▲ Page top
RE: Grim = Evil
QuoteReply
# Aug 20 2002 at 2:27 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591780307271
25 posts
Actually, my Sk guildie got this last night and PALA could use it. Pretty nice shoulder piece. Too bad I wasn't on my Pala when it dropped.

No evil clerics? what?
QuoteReply
# Aug 13 2001 at 8:55 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1595175348189
1 post
No such thing as an evil cleric? What? Have you never seen a Dark elf cleric, last I checked Inorruk was evil, being that inkie priests would worship Inorruk, kinda figures that they'd be evil too...
▲ Page top
grim pauldrons
QuoteReply
# Apr 13 2001 at 12:33 AM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Why would the fact that pallys can't wear this item be unbalancing? There are items that pallys can use that sks cannot. I have a marr's promise shield that i would like to use, but is pally only. Is that unbalancing? I don't really care, however...i have these on my sk and they are great!
▲ Page top
RE: grim pauldrons
QuoteReply
# Jul 06 2001 at 1:17 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591752153758
43 posts
Actually, Marr's promise shield is not Paladin only. But you are right, Shadow knights cannot use it because, it's a shield Blessed by Mithaniel Marr and shadow knights shouldn't be using an item that is soley used for people who don't raise the dead. So you keep your pauldrons and we'll keep our shield =). Good luck to you.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 05 2001 at 7:11 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) rofl, warriors can use, clerics can use, but a warrior/cleric (paladin) cannot use?
#Anonymous, Posted: Apr 05 2001 at 9:53 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I'm glad the pallys got "shafted" on this. I'm tired of them always ******** about wanting this and that to try and be more warrior like. I think Verant made a wise decision in this.
#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 09 2001 at 1:08 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I wish pallys couldn't wear dark mail guantlets either, unless the dark is the color and not the meaning behind the gloves.
RE: haha pallys get shafted
QuoteReply
# Jan 09 2001 at 1:07 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591749848700
4 posts
Pally probably can't use because they are supposed to be "good" and perhaps Verant sees "grim" pauldrons as "evil". I don't care that a high elf warrior can wear them, so don't go there. A paladin is "good" by class, not race.
